From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Basketball team representing Cameroon

Cameroon

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [FIBA ranking](/wiki/FIBA_Men%27s_World_Ranking "FIBA Men's World Ranking") | 59 Decrease 2 (3 March 2026)[[1]](#cite_note-FIBA_World_Rankings-1) |
| Joined [FIBA](/wiki/FIBA "FIBA") | 1965 |
| FIBA zone | [FIBA Africa](/wiki/FIBA_Africa "FIBA Africa") |
| National federation | [Cameroon Basketball Federation](/wiki/Cameroon_Basketball_Federation "Cameroon Basketball Federation") |
| [Coach](/wiki/Coach_(basketball) "Coach (basketball)") | [Alfred Aboya](/wiki/Alfred_Aboya "Alfred Aboya") |
| Nickname | Indomitable Lions |
| [AfroBasket](/wiki/AfroBasket "AfroBasket") | |
| Appearances | 11 |
| Medals | *Silver:* ([2007](/wiki/FIBA_Africa_Championship_2007 "FIBA Africa Championship 2007")) |
|  | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | Home jersey    Team colours  **[Home](/wiki/Basketball_uniform "Basketball uniform")** | Away jersey    Team colours  **[Away](/wiki/Basketball_uniform "Basketball uniform")** | | |
|  | |

The **Cameroon national basketball team** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Équipe nationale de basketball de Camerounaise*) represents [Cameroon](/wiki/Cameroon "Cameroon") in men's international competitions. It is administered by the [Fédération Camerounaise de Basketball](/w/index.php?title=F%C3%A9d%C3%A9ration_Camerounaise_de_Basketball&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fédération Camerounaise de Basketball (page does not exist)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Once an only average team, Cameroon has turned into a major force and contender for the title of the [African Basketball Championship](/wiki/African_Basketball_Championship "African Basketball Championship"). Their best achievement was reaching the Silver Medal in [2007](/wiki/FIBA_Africa_Championship "FIBA Africa Championship"). Since the late 2000s, the team has competed well against non-African competition, too.

At the [FIBA AfroBasket 2025](/wiki/FIBA_AfroBasket_2025 "FIBA AfroBasket 2025"), Cameroon reached the semifinals for a third time and finished fourth again.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Yves Missi](/wiki/Yves_Missi "Yves Missi") featured for the team, becoming the first active NBA player to represent the country since [Luc Mbah a Moute](/wiki/Luc_Mbah_a_Moute "Luc Mbah a Moute") in 2013.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Competitive record

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cameroon_men%27s_national_basketball_team&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Competitive record")]

### World Cup

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cameroon_men%27s_national_basketball_team&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: World Cup")]

Since 2019, FIBA organises qualification tournaments for African teams in order to qualify for the [FIBA Basketball World Cup](/wiki/FIBA_Basketball_World_Cup "FIBA Basketball World Cup").

| Total |  | | | | | 24 | 11 | 13 |

### Notable Cameroonian players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cameroon_men%27s_national_basketball_team&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Notable Cameroonian players")]

The following notable Cameroonian players are also eligible to represent the country in FIBA competitions:

* [Christian Koloko](/wiki/Christian_Koloko "Christian Koloko") (born 2000, C) – Player for the [Toronto Raptors](/wiki/Toronto_Raptors "Toronto Raptors").
* [Yves Missi](/wiki/Yves_Missi "Yves Missi") (born 2004, C) – Player for the [New Orleans Pelicans](/wiki/New_Orleans_Pelicans "New Orleans Pelicans").
* [Pascal Siakam](/wiki/Pascal_Siakam "Pascal Siakam") (born 1994, F) – Player for the [Indiana Pacers](/wiki/Indiana_Pacers "Indiana Pacers").
* [Ulrich Chomche](/wiki/Ulrich_Chomche "Ulrich Chomche") (born 2005, C) - Player for the [Toronto Raptors](/wiki/Toronto_Raptors "Toronto Raptors")
